content_markdown:
In the diagram the voltmeter V has a resistance of $100\text{ k}\Omega$ and is connected such as to measure the potential difference across the $100\text{ k}\Omega$ resistor. The battery has an emf of $5.0\text{ V}$ and negligible internal resistance.

The reading on the voltmeter will be

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a 5.0 V battery connected in series with a 200 kOhm resistor and a parallel combination of a 100 kOhm resistor and a voltmeter with 100 kOhm resistance.

vibe_explanation: First, calculate the equivalent resistance of the parallel section (the $100\text{ k}\Omega$ resistor and the $100\text{ k}\Omega$ voltmeter). Two identical resistors in parallel have a combined resistance of half their value, so $R_p = 50\text{ k}\Omega$. Now, the circuit is a potential divider with a $200\text{ k}\Omega$ resistor in series with a $50\text{ k}\Omega$ equivalent resistance. The total resistance is $250\text{ k}\Omega$. The voltage across the parallel section (the voltmeter reading) is $V = 5.0\text{ V} \times \frac{50\text{ k}\Omega}{200\text{ k}\Omega + 50\text{ k}\Omega} = 5.0 \times \frac{50}{250} = 5.0 \times \frac{1}{5} = 1.0\text{ V}$.
